description: All vehicles entering Joshua Tree National Park must pay an entrance fee or hold an America the Beautiful pass. No day-use permits are currently required.
where: Pay at entrance stations or purchase online
cost: $30 per vehicle (7-day pass) · $55 annual park pass · $80 America the Beautiful annual pass

seasonalWindow.notes: Fee applies year-round at all three entrance stations (West, North, South).

tips: The America the Beautiful pass ($80/year) covers all national parks and is worth it if you visit two or more parks.
Fee-free days occur several times a year — check NPS calendar.
Self-service fee stations at the west entrance can have long lines on spring weekends — arrive early.
The north entrance at Twentynine Palms is typically less crowded.

description: Some campgrounds (Black Rock, Indian Cove, Cottonwood) accept or require reservations via recreation.gov. Others (Jumbo Rocks, Hidden Valley, White Tank, Ryan, Belle) are first-come first-served only.

seasonalWindow.notes: Peak demand in spring (Mar–May) and fall (Oct–Nov). First-come sites fill by late morning on weekends.

tips: Black Rock and Indian Cove are reservable up to 6 months ahead — book early for peak weekends.
First-come campgrounds (Jumbo Rocks, Hidden Valley, White Tank) require arriving early — by 10 AM on spring/fall weekends.
Most campgrounds have no water — fill up at the Oasis Visitor Center in Twentynine Palms or Black Rock.
Generator hours are limited in all campgrounds — quiet hours are enforced.

description: All climbers and boulderers should register at the park entrance. Chalk use is regulated in some zones. Leave No Trace standards apply to all climbing.
where: Park entrance station or visitor center

seasonalWindow.notes: Registration required year-round. Fixed anchors and bolt placement regulated.

tips: Register at the entrance station — it helps search and rescue locate you if needed.
Chalk must be earth-toned or neutral in many areas. Check current NPS regulations.
Fixed anchor and bolt placement is strictly regulated — review NPS climbing regulations before placing hardware.
Popular climbing areas (Intersection Rock, Hidden Valley) get crowded on weekends — arrive early.
